arguments.message: I'll add the three profile fields. Should the timezone default to 'UTC'? And should avatar_url have a length limit?

turns.content: Yes, default UTC for timezone. Avatar URL max 500 chars.

arguments.content:
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime
from database import Base
from datetime import datetime


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True)
    email = Column(String(200), unique=True)
    bio = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    avatar_url = Column(String(500), nullable=True)
    timezone = Column(String(50), default='UTC')
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

"""Add profile fields: bio, avatar_url, timezone

Revision ID: 002_profile_fields
"""
import sqlalchemy as sa
from alembic import op


def upgrade():
    op.add_column('users', sa.Column('bio', sa.Text(), nullable=True))
    op.add_column('users', sa.Column('avatar_url', sa.String(500), nullable=True))
    op.add_column('users', sa.Column('timezone', sa.String(50), server_default='UTC'))


def downgrade():
    op.drop_column('users', 'timezone')
    op.drop_column('users', 'avatar_url')
    op.drop_column('users', 'bio')

arguments.content:
return jsonify({
        'id': user.id,
        'username': user.username,
        'email': user.email,
        'bio': user.bio,
        'avatar_url': user.avatar_url,
        'timezone': user.timezone,
    })

arguments.message: Profile fields added to the model, API response, and migration. Run `alembic upgrade head` to apply. All new fields are nullable except timezone which defaults to 'UTC'. I also updated the GET /api/users/<id> response to include the new fields.
